result.summary: Found 4 issues: multiple layout clipping problems where game cards and navigation items are cut off at the right edge of the screen, indicating overflow/clipping issues with the grid and navigation components.

issues.description: The rightmost column of game cards is consistently clipped/cut off across both the 'BetRivers Exclusive Games' and 'Top Played New Games' sections. Game titles like 'MAGNI PHO...', 'MUNC MI...', 'HYPERC...', and 'DAR WATE...' are partially visible, indicating horizontal overflow without proper containment.
issues.suggestion: Ensure the game card grid uses proper CSS overflow handling (overflow: hidden on container) or adjust the grid column sizing so the last visible column doesn't bleed beyond the viewport boundary. Consider using a carousel or scroll indicator to signal more content exists.

issues.description: The top navigation tab 'Hot Games' is clipped at the right edge, showing only 'Hot Ga', which makes it appear broken and reduces usability.
issues.suggestion: Implement a horizontal scrollable tab bar with a visible scroll indicator or fade-out gradient on the right edge to clearly communicate that more tabs exist and are scrollable.

issues.description: The 'Cop the Lot' game card displays a jackpot amount ($6,268.60) but other cards in the same section do not, creating visual inconsistency in the card layout.
issues.suggestion: Standardize game card templates — either show jackpot amounts on all eligible cards or use a consistent badge/label system to differentiate jackpot games from non-jackpot ones.

issues.description: The 'i' (info) icon buttons on game cards appear very small and low contrast against the dark card backgrounds, potentially making them difficult to tap or notice for users with visual impairments.
issues.suggestion: Increase the size and contrast of the info icon buttons to meet minimum touch target size (44x44px) and ensure sufficient contrast ratio per WCAG 2.1 guidelines.
